query:
You are working on the Kotlin/kotlinx.coroutines repository.
The codebase is at version 1.6 (commit: 67e21b24).

## Issue to Resolve

CoroutineScope.future() may trigger global exception handler on cancellation
### Expected behavior

`future` coroutine builder delivers all errors via the returned object, just like `async`.

### Actual behavior

`future` coroutine builder can suddenly trigger global exception handler that may crash the application.

Sample code:
```kotlin
fun main() {
    val future = GlobalScope.future {
        error("foo")
    }
    future.cancel(true)
    Thread.sleep(100)
}
```

Result:
`Exception in thread "DefaultDispatcher-worker-1" java.lang.IllegalStateException: foo`

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

I believe `CompletableFutureCorotine` should not attempt to handle the exception in any other way than completing the returned `CompletableFuture`. For now, [this is not the case](https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx.coroutines/blob/d88a8c227a295f91251960c8fe6b21a02ddf704b/integration/kotlinx-coroutines-jdk8/src/future/Future.kt#L63).

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `kotlinx-coroutines-core/jdk8/src/future/Future.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

conversation_history.name: read

content.thinking:
**Analyzing coroutine cancellation and future completion conflicts**

I'm untangling a tricky concurrency problem where canceling a future that wraps a coroutine can cause exceptions to leak globally if the future is already completed or canceled. I want to adjust cancellation handling so that if the future is already canceled, we avoid triggering additional global error handlers, ensuring errors are properly delivered only through the future result as expected. I'll consider tweaking the onCancelled logic to suppress unnecessary global exceptions while preserving correct error reporting.

conversation_history.content:
100â†’ * If you have to use the `onCompletion` operator, please report to https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx.coroutines/issues/.
101â†’ * As a temporary solution, [invokeOnCompletion][Job.invokeOnCompletion] can be used instead:
102â†’ * ```
103â†’ * fun <E> ReceiveChannel<E>.myOperator(): ReceiveChannel<E> = GlobalScope.produce(Dispatchers.Unconfined) {
104â†’ *     coroutineContext[Job]?.invokeOnCompletion { consumes() }
105â†’ * }
106â†’ * ```
107â†’ * @suppress
108â†’ */
109â†’@InternalCoroutinesApi
110â†’public fun <E> CoroutineScope.produce(
111â†’    context: CoroutineContext = EmptyCoroutineContext,
112â†’    capacity: Int = 0,
113â†’    start: CoroutineStart = CoroutineStart.DEFAULT,
114â†’    onCompletion: CompletionHandler? = null,
115â†’    @BuilderInference block: suspend ProducerScope<E>.() -> Unit
116â†’): ReceiveChannel<E> =
117â†’    produce(context, capacity, BufferOverflow.SUSPEND, start, onCompletion, block)
118â†’
119â†’// Internal version of produce that is maximally flexible, but is not exposed through public API (too many params)
120â†’internal fun <E> CoroutineScope.produce(
121â†’    context: CoroutineContext = EmptyCoroutineContext,
122â†’    capacity: Int = 0,
123â†’    onBufferOverflow: BufferOverflow = BufferOverflow.SUSPEND,
124â†’    start: CoroutineStart = CoroutineStart.DEFAULT,
125â†’    onCompletion: CompletionHandler? = null,
126â†’    @BuilderInference block: suspend ProducerScope<E>.() -> Unit
127â†’): ReceiveChannel<E> {
128â†’    val channel = Channel<E>(capacity, onBufferOverflow)
129â†’    val newContext = newCoroutineContext(context)
130â†’    val coroutine = ProducerCoroutine(newContext, channel)
131â†’    if (onCompletion != null) coroutine.invokeOnCompletion(handler = onCompletion)
132â†’    coroutine.start(start, coroutine, block)
133â†’    return coroutine
134â†’}
135â†’
136â†’private class ProducerCoroutine<E>(
137â†’    parentContext: CoroutineContext, channel: Channel<E>
138â†’) : ChannelCoroutine<E>(parentContext, channel, true, active = true), ProducerScope<E> {
139â†’    override val isActive: Boolean
140â†’        get() = super.isActive
141â†’
142â†’    override fun onCompleted(value: Unit) {
143â†’        _channel.close()
144â†’    }
145â†’
146â†’    override fun onCancelled(cause: Throwable, handled: Boolean) {
147â†’        val processed = _channel.close(cause)
148â†’        if (!processed && !handled) handleCoroutineException(context, cause)
149â†’    }
150â†’}
151â†’

conversation_history.content:
1â†’/*
  2â†’ * Copyright 2016-2021 JetBrains s.r.o. Use of this source code is governed by the Apache 2.0 license.
  3â†’ */
  4â†’
  5â†’@file:JvmMultifileClass
  6â†’@file:JvmName("BuildersKt")
  7â†’@file:OptIn(ExperimentalContracts::class)
  8â†’
  9â†’package kotlinx.coroutines
 10â†’
 11â†’import kotlinx.atomicfu.*
 12â†’import kotlinx.coroutines.internal.*
 13â†’import kotlinx.coroutines.intrinsics.*
 14â†’import kotlinx.coroutines.selects.*
 15â†’import kotlin.contracts.*
 16â†’import kotlin.coroutines.*
 17â†’import kotlin.coroutines.intrinsics.*
 18â†’import kotlin.jvm.*
 19â†’
 20â†’// --------------- launch ---------------
 21â†’
 22â†’/**
 23â†’ * Launches a new coroutine without blocking the current thread and returns a reference to the coroutine as a [Job].
 24â†’ * The coroutine is cancelled when the resulting job is [cancelled][Job.cancel].
 25â†’ *
 26â†’ * The coroutine context is inherited from a [CoroutineScope]. Additional context elements can be specified with [context] argument.
 27â†’ * If the context does not have any dispatcher nor any other [ContinuationInterceptor], then [Dispatchers.Default] is used.
 28â†’ * The parent job is inherited from a [CoroutineScope] as well, but it can also be overridden
 29â†’ * with a corresponding [context] element.
 30â†’ *
 31â†’ * By default, the coroutine is immediately scheduled for execution.
 32â†’ * Other start options can be specified via `start` parameter. See [CoroutineStart] for details.
 33â†’ * An optional [start] parameter can be set to [CoroutineStart.LAZY] to start coroutine _lazily_. In this case,
 34â†’ * the coroutine [Job] is created in _new_ state. It can be explicitly started with [start][Job.start] function
 35â†’ * and will be started implicitly on the first invocation of [join][Job.join].
 36â†’ *
 37â†’ * Uncaught exceptions in this coroutine cancel the parent job in the context by default
 38â†’ * (unless [CoroutineExceptionHandler] is explicitly specified), which means that when `launch` is used with
 39â†’ * the context of another coroutine, then any uncaught exception leads to the cancellation of the parent coroutine.
 40â†’ *
 41â†’ * See [newCoroutineContext] for a description of debugging facilities that are available for a newly created coroutine.
 42â†’ *
 43â†’ * @param context additional to [CoroutineScope.coroutineContext] context of the coroutine.
 44â†’ * @param start coroutine start option. The default value is [CoroutineStart.DEFAULT].
 45â†’ * @param block the coroutine code which will be invoked in the context of the provided scope.
 46â†’ **/
 47â†’public fun CoroutineScope.launch(
 48â†’    context: CoroutineContext = EmptyCoroutineContext,
 49â†’    start: CoroutineStart = CoroutineStart.DEFAULT,
 50â†’    block: suspend CoroutineScope.() -> Unit
 51â†’): Job {
 52â†’    val newContext = newCoroutineContext(context)
 53â†’    val coroutine = if (start.isLazy)
 54â†’        LazyStandaloneCoroutine(newContext, block) else
 55â†’        StandaloneCoroutine(newContext, active = true)
 56â†’    coroutine.start(start, coroutine, block)
 57â†’    return coroutine
 58â†’}
 59â†’
 60â†’// --------------- async ---------------
 61â†’
 62â†’/**
 63â†’ * Creates a coroutine and returns its future result as an implementation of [Deferred].
 64â†’ * The running coroutine is cancelled when the resulting deferred is [cancelled][Job.cancel].
 65â†’ * The resulting coroutine has a key difference compared with similar primitives in other languages
 66â†’ * and frameworks: it cancels the parent job (or outer scope) on failure to enforce *structured concurrency* paradigm.
 67â†’ * To change that behaviour, supervising parent ([SupervisorJob] or [supervisorScope]) can be used.
 68â†’ *
 69â†’ * Coroutine context is inherited from a [CoroutineScope], additional context elements can be specified with [context] argument.
 70â†’ * If the context does not have any dispatcher nor any other [ContinuationInterceptor], then [Dispatchers.Default] is used.
 71â†’ * The parent job is inherited from a [CoroutineScope] as well, but it can also be overridden
 72â†’ * with corresponding [context] element.
 73â†’ *
 74â†’ * By default, the coroutine is immediately scheduled for execution.
 75â†’ * Other options can be specified via `start` parameter. See [CoroutineStart] for details.
 76â†’ * An optional [start] parameter can be set to [CoroutineStart.LAZY] to start coroutine _lazily_. In this case,
 77â†’ * the resulting [Deferred] is created in _new_ state. It can be explicitly started with [start][Job.start]
 78â†’ * function and will be started implicitly on the first invocation of [join][Job.join], [await][Deferred.await] or [awaitAll].
 79â†’ *
 80â†’ * @param block the coroutine code.
 81â†’ */
 82â†’public fun <T> CoroutineScope.async(
 83â†’    context: CoroutineContext = EmptyCoroutineContext,
 84â†’    start: CoroutineStart = CoroutineStart.DEFAULT,
 85â†’    block: suspend CoroutineScope.() -> T
 86â†’): Deferred<T> {
 87â†’    val newContext = newCoroutineContext(context)
 88â†’    val coroutine = if (start.isLazy)
 89â†’        LazyDeferredCoroutine(newContext, block) else
 90â†’        DeferredCoroutine<T>(newContext, active = true)
 91â†’    coroutine.start(start, coroutine, block)
 92â†’    return coroutine
 93â†’}
 94â†’
 95â†’@Suppress("UNCHECKED_CAST")
 96â†’private open class DeferredCoroutine<T>(
 97â†’    parentContext: CoroutineContext,
 98â†’    active: Boolean
 99â†’) : AbstractCoroutine<T>(parentContext, true, active = active), Deferred<T> {
100â†’    override fun getCompleted(): T = getCompletedInternal() as T
101â†’    override suspend fun await(): T = awaitInternal() as T
102â†’    override val onAwait: SelectClause1<T> get() = onAwaitInternal as SelectClause1<T>
103â†’}
104â†’
105â†’private class LazyDeferredCoroutine<T>(
106â†’    parentContext: CoroutineContext,
107â†’    block: suspend CoroutineScope.() -> T
108â†’) : DeferredCoroutine<T>(parentContext, active = false) {
109â†’    private val continuation = block.createCoroutineUnintercepted(this, this)
110â†’
111â†’    override fun onStart() {
112â†’        continuation.startCoroutineCancellable(this)
113â†’    }
114â†’}
115â†’
116â†’// --------------- withContext ---------------
117â†’
118â†’/**
119â†’ * Calls the specified suspending block with a given coroutine context, suspends until it completes, and returns
120â†’ * the result.
121â†’ *
122â†’ * The resulting context for the [block] is derived by merging the current [coroutineContext] with the
123â†’ * specified [context] using `coroutineContext + context` (see [CoroutineContext.plus]).
124â†’ * This suspending function is cancellable. It immediately checks for cancellation of
125â†’ * the resulting context and throws [CancellationException] if it is not [active][CoroutineContext.isActive].
126â†’ *
127â†’ * Calls to [withContext] whose [context] argument provides a [CoroutineDispatcher] that is
128â†’ * different from the current one, by necessity, perform additional dispatches: the [block]
129â†’ * can not be executed immediately and needs to be dispatched for execution on
130â†’ * the passed [CoroutineDispatcher], and then when the [block] completes, the execution
131â†’ * has to shift back to the original dispatcher.
132â†’ *
133â†’ * Note that the result of `withContext` invocation is dispatched into the original context in a cancellable way
134â†’ * with a **prompt cancellation guarantee**, which means that if the original [coroutineContext]
135â†’ * in which `withContext` was invoked is cancelled by the time its dispatcher starts to execute the code,
136â†’ * it discards the result of `withContext` and throws [CancellationException].
137â†’ *
138â†’ * The cancellation behaviour described above is enabled if and only if the dispatcher is being changed.
139â†’ * For example, when using `withContext(NonCancellable) { ... }` there is no change in dispatcher and
140â†’ * this call will not be cancelled neither on entry to the block inside `withContext` nor on exit from it.
141â†’ */
142â†’public suspend fun <T> withContext(
143â†’    context: CoroutineContext,
144â†’    block: suspend CoroutineScope.() -> T
145â†’): T {
146â†’    contract {
147â†’        callsInPlace(block, InvocationKind.EXACTLY_ONCE)
148â†’    }
149â†’    return suspendCoroutineUninterceptedOrReturn sc@ { uCont ->
150â†’        // compute new context
151â†’        val oldContext = uCont.context
152â†’        // Copy CopyableThreadContextElement if necessary
153â†’        val newContext = oldContext.newCoroutineContext(context)
154â†’        // always check for cancellation of new context
155â†’        newContext.ensureActive()
156â†’        // FAST PATH #1 -- new context is the same as the old one
157â†’        if (newContext === oldContext) {
158â†’            val coroutine = ScopeCoroutine(newContext, uCont)
159â†’            return@sc coroutine.startUndispatchedOrReturn(coroutine, block)
160â†’        }
161â†’        // FAST PATH #2 -- the new dispatcher is the same as the old one (something else changed)
162â†’        // `equals` is used by design (see equals implementation is wrapper context like ExecutorCoroutineDispatcher)
163â†’        if (newContext[ContinuationInterceptor] == oldContext[ContinuationInterceptor]) {
164â†’            val coroutine = UndispatchedCoroutine(newContext, uCont)
165â†’            // There are changes in the context, so this thread needs to be updated
166â†’            withCoroutineContext(coroutine.context, null) {
167â†’                return@sc coroutine.startUndispatchedOrReturn(coroutine, block)
168â†’            }
169â†’        }
170â†’        // SLOW PATH -- use new dispatcher
171â†’        val coroutine = DispatchedCoroutine(newContext, uCont)
172â†’        block.startCoroutineCancellable(coroutine, coroutine)
173â†’        coroutine.getResult()
174â†’    }
175â†’}
176â†’
177â†’/**
178â†’ * Calls the specified suspending block with the given [CoroutineDispatcher], suspends until it
179â†’ * completes, and returns the result.
180â†’ *
181â†’ * This inline function calls [withContext].
182â†’ */
183â†’public suspend inline operator fun <T> CoroutineDispatcher.invoke(
184â†’    noinline block: suspend CoroutineScope.() -> T
185â†’): T = withContext(this, block)
186â†’
187â†’// --------------- implementation ---------------
188â†’
189â†’private open class StandaloneCoroutine(
190â†’    parentContext: CoroutineContext,
191â†’    active: Boolean
192â†’) : AbstractCoroutine<Unit>(parentContext, initParentJob = true, active = active) {
193â†’    override fun handleJobException(exception: Throwable): Boolean {
194â†’        handleCoroutineException(context, exception)
195â†’        return true
196â†’    }
197â†’}
198â†’
199â†’private class LazyStandaloneCoroutine(
200â†’    parentContext: CoroutineContext,
201â†’    block: suspend CoroutineScope.() -> Unit
202â†’) : StandaloneCoroutine(parentContext, active = false) {
203â†’    private val continuation = block.createCoroutineUnintercepted(this, this)
204â†’
205â†’    override fun onStart() {
206â†’        continuation.startCoroutineCancellable(this)
207â†’    }
208â†’}
209â†’
210â†’// Used by withContext when context changes, but dispatcher stays the same
211â†’internal expect class UndispatchedCoroutine<in T>(
212â†’    context: CoroutineContext,
213â†’    uCont: Continuation<T>
214â†’) : ScopeCoroutine<T>
215â†’
216â†’private const val UNDECIDED = 0
217â†’private const val SUSPENDED = 1
218â†’private const val RESUMED = 2
219â†’
220â†’// Used by withContext when context dispatcher changes
221â†’internal class DispatchedCoroutine<in T>(
222â†’    context: CoroutineContext,
223â†’    uCont: Continuation<T>
224â†’) : ScopeCoroutine<T>(context, uCont) {
225â†’    // this is copy-and-paste of a decision state machine inside AbstractionContinuation
226â†’    // todo: we may some-how abstract it via inline class
227â†’    private val _decision = atomic(UNDECIDED)
228â†’
229â†’    private fun trySuspend(): Boolean {
230â†’        _decision.loop { decision ->
231â†’            when (decision) {
232â†’                UNDECIDED -> if (this._decision.compareAndSet(UNDECIDED, SUSPENDED)) return true
233â†’                RESUMED -> return false
234â†’                else -> error("Already suspended")
235â†’            }
236â†’        }
237â†’    }
238â†’
239â†’    private fun tryResume(): Boolean {
240â†’        _decision.loop { decision ->
241â†’            when (decision) {
242â†’                UNDECIDED -> if (this._decision.compareAndSet(UNDECIDED, RESUMED)) return true
243â†’                SUSPENDED -> return false
244â†’                else -> error("Already resumed")
245â†’            }
246â†’        }
247â†’    }
248â†’
249â†’    override fun afterCompletion(state: Any?) {
250â†’        // Call afterResume from afterCompletion and not vice-versa, because stack-size is more
251â†’        // important for afterResume implementation
252â†’        afterResume(state)
253â†’    }
254â†’
255â†’    override fun afterResume(state: Any?) {
256â†’        if (tryResume()) return // completed before getResult invocation -- bail out
257â†’        // Resume in a cancellable way because we have to switch back to the original dispatcher
258â†’        uCont.intercepted().resumeCancellableWith(recoverResult(state, uCont))
259â†’    }
260â†’
261â†’    fun getResult(): Any? {
262â†’        if (trySuspend()) return COROUTINE_SUSPENDED
263â†’        // otherwise, onCompletionInternal was already invoked & invoked tryResume, and the result is in the state
264â†’        val state = this.state.unboxState()
265â†’        if (state is CompletedExceptionally) throw state.cause
266â†’        @Suppress("UNCHECKED_CAST")
267â†’        return state as T
268â†’    }
269â†’}
270â†’

content.thinking:
**Refining onCancelled logic in CompletableFutureCoroutine**

I'm working through the complexities of the `onCancelled` method to prevent unnecessary global exception handling when a future is already completed or cancelled. I want to ensure exceptions are only escalated if the future completed normally without errors and the cancellation wasn't handled, carefully balancing avoiding lost exceptions versus noisy error reports. I'll adjust the method to check the futureâ€™s state after attempting to complete it exceptionally and only call the global handler when truly necessary.

content.text:
Summary:
- Updated `CompletableFutureCoroutine.onCancelled` so that exceptions are only escalated to the global handler when the associated `CompletableFuture` hasnâ€™t already been completed, completed exceptionally, or cancelled.
- This ensures that cancelling the future (or any other external completion) no longer triggers the global exception handler while still surfacing truly unhandled failures.

Testing:
- Not run (not requested).

100â†’ * If you have to use the `onCompletion` operator, please report to https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx.coroutines/issues/.
101â†’ * As a temporary solution, [invokeOnCompletion][Job.invokeOnCompletion] can be used instead:
102â†’ * ```
103â†’ * fun <E> ReceiveChannel<E>.myOperator(): ReceiveChannel<E> = GlobalScope.produce(Dispatchers.Unconfined) {
104â†’ *     coroutineContext[Job]?.invokeOnCompletion { consumes() }
105â†’ * }
106â†’ * ```
107â†’ * @suppress
108â†’ */
109â†’@InternalCoroutinesApi
110â†’public fun <E> CoroutineScope.produce(
111â†’    context: CoroutineContext = EmptyCoroutineContext,
112â†’    capacity: Int = 0,
113â†’    start: CoroutineStart = CoroutineStart.DEFAULT,
114â†’    onCompletion: CompletionHandler? = null,
115â†’    @BuilderInference block: suspend ProducerScope<E>.() -> Unit
116â†’): ReceiveChannel<E> =
117â†’    produce(context, capacity, BufferOverflow.SUSPEND, start, onCompletion, block)
118â†’
119â†’// Internal version of produce that is maximally flexible, but is not exposed through public API (too many params)
120â†’internal fun <E> CoroutineScope.produce(
121â†’    context: CoroutineContext = EmptyCoroutineContext,
122â†’    capacity: Int = 0,
123â†’    onBufferOverflow: BufferOverflow = BufferOverflow.SUSPEND,
124â†’    start: CoroutineStart = CoroutineStart.DEFAULT,
125â†’    onCompletion: CompletionHandler? = null,
126â†’    @BuilderInference block: suspend ProducerScope<E>.() -> Unit
127â†’): ReceiveChannel<E> {
128â†’    val channel = Channel<E>(capacity, onBufferOverflow)
129â†’    val newContext = newCoroutineContext(context)
130â†’    val coroutine = ProducerCoroutine(newContext, channel)
131â†’    if (onCompletion != null) coroutine.invokeOnCompletion(handler = onCompletion)
132â†’    coroutine.start(start, coroutine, block)
133â†’    return coroutine
134â†’}
135â†’
136â†’private class ProducerCoroutine<E>(
137â†’    parentContext: CoroutineContext, channel: Channel<E>
138â†’) : ChannelCoroutine<E>(parentContext, channel, true, active = true), ProducerScope<E> {
139â†’    override val isActive: Boolean
140â†’        get() = super.isActive
141â†’
142â†’    override fun onCompleted(value: Unit) {
143â†’        _channel.close()
144â†’    }
145â†’
146â†’    override fun onCancelled(cause: Throwable, handled: Boolean) {
147â†’        val processed = _channel.close(cause)
148â†’        if (!processed && !handled) handleCoroutineException(context, cause)
149â†’    }
150â†’}

1â†’/*
  2â†’ * Copyright 2016-2021 JetBrains s.r.o. Use of this source code is governed by the Apache 2.0 license.
  3â†’ */
  4â†’
  5â†’@file:JvmMultifileClass
  6â†’@file:JvmName("BuildersKt")
  7â†’@file:OptIn(ExperimentalContracts::class)
  8â†’
  9â†’package kotlinx.coroutines
 10â†’
 11â†’import kotlinx.atomicfu.*
 12â†’import kotlinx.coroutines.internal.*
 13â†’import kotlinx.coroutines.intrinsics.*
 14â†’import kotlinx.coroutines.selects.*
 15â†’import kotlin.contracts.*
 16â†’import kotlin.coroutines.*
 17â†’import kotlin.coroutines.intrinsics.*
 18â†’import kotlin.jvm.*
 19â†’
 20â†’// --------------- launch ---------------
 21â†’
 22â†’/**
 23â†’ * Launches a new coroutine without blocking the current thread and returns a reference to the coroutine as a [Job].
 24â†’ * The coroutine is cancelled when the resulting job is [cancelled][Job.cancel].
 25â†’ *
 26â†’ * The coroutine context is inherited from a [CoroutineScope]. Additional context elements can be specified with [context] argument.
 27â†’ * If the context does not have any dispatcher nor any other [ContinuationInterceptor], then [Dispatchers.Default] is used.
 28â†’ * The parent job is inherited from a [CoroutineScope] as well, but it can also be overridden
 29â†’ * with a corresponding [context] element.
 30â†’ *
 31â†’ * By default, the coroutine is immediately scheduled for execution.
 32â†’ * Other start options can be specified via `start` parameter. See [CoroutineStart] for details.
 33â†’ * An optional [start] parameter can be set to [CoroutineStart.LAZY] to start coroutine _lazily_. In this case,
 34â†’ * the coroutine [Job] is created in _new_ state. It can be explicitly started with [start][Job.start] function
 35â†’ * and will be started implicitly on the first invocation of [join][Job.join].
 36â†’ *
 37â†’ * Uncaught exceptions in this coroutine cancel the parent job in the context by default
 38â†’ * (unless [CoroutineExceptionHandler] is explicitly specified), which means that when `launch` is used with
 39â†’ * the context of another coroutine, then any uncaught exception leads to the cancellation of the parent coroutine.
 40â†’ *
 41â†’ * See [newCoroutineContext] for a description of debugging facilities that are available for a newly created coroutine.
 42â†’ *
 43â†’ * @param context additional to [CoroutineScope.coroutineContext] context of the coroutine.
 44â†’ * @param start coroutine start option. The default value is [CoroutineStart.DEFAULT].
 45â†’ * @param block the coroutine code which will be invoked in the context of the provided scope.
 46â†’ **/
 47â†’public fun CoroutineScope.launch(
 48â†’    context: CoroutineContext = EmptyCoroutineContext,
 49â†’    start: CoroutineStart = CoroutineStart.DEFAULT,
 50â†’    block: suspend CoroutineScope.() -> Unit
 51â†’): Job {
 52â†’    val newContext = newCoroutineContext(context)
 53â†’    val coroutine = if (start.isLazy)
 54â†’        LazyStandaloneCoroutine(newContext, block) else
 55â†’        StandaloneCoroutine(newContext, active = true)
 56â†’    coroutine.start(start, coroutine, block)
 57â†’    return coroutine
 58â†’}
 59â†’
 60â†’// --------------- async ---------------
 61â†’
 62â†’/**
 63â†’ * Creates a coroutine and returns its future result as an implementation of [Deferred].
 64â†’ * The running coroutine is cancelled when the resulting deferred is [cancelled][Job.cancel].
 65â†’ * The resulting coroutine has a key difference compared with similar primitives in other languages
 66â†’ * and frameworks: it cancels the parent job (or outer scope) on failure to enforce *structured concurrency* paradigm.
 67â†’ * To change that behaviour, supervising parent ([SupervisorJob] or [supervisorScope]) can be used.
 68â†’ *
 69â†’ * Coroutine context is inherited from a [CoroutineScope], additional context elements can be specified with [context] argument.
 70â†’ * If the context does not have any dispatcher nor any other [ContinuationInterceptor], then [Dispatchers.Default] is used.
 71â†’ * The parent job is inherited from a [CoroutineScope] as well, but it can also be overridden
 72â†’ * with corresponding [context] element.
 73â†’ *
 74â†’ * By default, the coroutine is immediately scheduled for execution.
 75â†’ * Other options can be specified via `start` parameter. See [CoroutineStart] for details.
 76â†’ * An optional [start] parameter can be set to [CoroutineStart.LAZY] to start coroutine _lazily_. In this case,
 77â†’ * the resulting [Deferred] is created in _new_ state. It can be explicitly started with [start][Job.start]
 78â†’ * function and will be started implicitly on the first invocation of [join][Job.join], [await][Deferred.await] or [awaitAll].
 79â†’ *
 80â†’ * @param block the coroutine code.
 81â†’ */
 82â†’public fun <T> CoroutineScope.async(
 83â†’    context: CoroutineContext = EmptyCoroutineContext,
 84â†’    start: CoroutineStart = CoroutineStart.DEFAULT,
 85â†’    block: suspend CoroutineScope.() -> T
 86â†’): Deferred<T> {
 87â†’    val newContext = newCoroutineContext(context)
 88â†’    val coroutine = if (start.isLazy)
 89â†’        LazyDeferredCoroutine(newContext, block) else
 90â†’        DeferredCoroutine<T>(newContext, active = true)
 91â†’    coroutine.start(start, coroutine, block)
 92â†’    return coroutine
 93â†’}
 94â†’
 95â†’@Suppress("UNCHECKED_CAST")
 96â†’private open class DeferredCoroutine<T>(
 97â†’    parentContext: CoroutineContext,
 98â†’    active: Boolean
 99â†’) : AbstractCoroutine<T>(parentContext, true, active = active), Deferred<T> {
100â†’    override fun getCompleted(): T = getCompletedInternal() as T
101â†’    override suspend fun await(): T = awaitInternal() as T
102â†’    override val onAwait: SelectClause1<T> get() = onAwaitInternal as SelectClause1<T>
103â†’}
104â†’
105â†’private class LazyDeferredCoroutine<T>(
106â†’    parentContext: CoroutineContext,
107â†’    block: suspend CoroutineScope.() -> T
108â†’) : DeferredCoroutine<T>(parentContext, active = false) {
109â†’    private val continuation = block.createCoroutineUnintercepted(this, this)
110â†’
111â†’    override fun onStart() {
112â†’        continuation.startCoroutineCancellable(this)
113â†’    }
114â†’}
115â†’
116â†’// --------------- withContext ---------------
117â†’
118â†’/**
119â†’ * Calls the specified suspending block with a given coroutine context, suspends until it completes, and returns
120â†’ * the result.
121â†’ *
122â†’ * The resulting context for the [block] is derived by merging the current [coroutineContext] with the
123â†’ * specified [context] using `coroutineContext + context` (see [CoroutineContext.plus]).
124â†’ * This suspending function is cancellable. It immediately checks for cancellation of
125â†’ * the resulting context and throws [CancellationException] if it is not [active][CoroutineContext.isActive].
126â†’ *
127â†’ * Calls to [withContext] whose [context] argument provides a [CoroutineDispatcher] that is
128â†’ * different from the current one, by necessity, perform additional dispatches: the [block]
129â†’ * can not be executed immediately and needs to be dispatched for execution on
130â†’ * the passed [CoroutineDispatcher], and then when the [block] completes, the execution
131â†’ * has to shift back to the original dispatcher.
132â†’ *
133â†’ * Note that the result of `withContext` invocation is dispatched into the original context in a cancellable way
134â†’ * with a **prompt cancellation guarantee**, which means that if the original [coroutineContext]
135â†’ * in which `withContext` was invoked is cancelled by the time its dispatcher starts to execute the code,
136â†’ * it discards the result of `withContext` and throws [CancellationException].
137â†’ *
138â†’ * The cancellation behaviour described above is enabled if and only if the dispatcher is being changed.
139â†’ * For example, when using `withContext(NonCancellable) { ... }` there is no change in dispatcher and
140â†’ * this call will not be cancelled neither on entry to the block inside `withContext` nor on exit from it.
141â†’ */
142â†’public suspend fun <T> withContext(
143â†’    context: CoroutineContext,
144â†’    block: suspend CoroutineScope.() -> T
145â†’): T {
146â†’    contract {
147â†’        callsInPlace(block, InvocationKind.EXACTLY_ONCE)
148â†’    }
149â†’    return suspendCoroutineUninterceptedOrReturn sc@ { uCont ->
150â†’        // compute new context
151â†’        val oldContext = uCont.context
152â†’        // Copy CopyableThreadContextElement if necessary
153â†’        val newContext = oldContext.newCoroutineContext(context)
154â†’        // always check for cancellation of new context
155â†’        newContext.ensureActive()
156â†’        // FAST PATH #1 -- new context is the same as the old one
157â†’        if (newContext === oldContext) {
158â†’            val coroutine = ScopeCoroutine(newContext, uCont)
159â†’            return@sc coroutine.startUndispatchedOrReturn(coroutine, block)
160â†’        }
161â†’        // FAST PATH #2 -- the new dispatcher is the same as the old one (something else changed)
162â†’        // `equals` is used by design (see equals implementation is wrapper context like ExecutorCoroutineDispatcher)
163â†’        if (newContext[ContinuationInterceptor] == oldContext[ContinuationInterceptor]) {
164â†’            val coroutine = UndispatchedCoroutine(newContext, uCont)
165â†’            // There are changes in the context, so this thread needs to be updated
166â†’            withCoroutineContext(coroutine.context, null) {
167â†’                return@sc coroutine.startUndispatchedOrReturn(coroutine, block)
168â†’            }
169â†’        }
170â†’        // SLOW PATH -- use new dispatcher
171â†’        val coroutine = DispatchedCoroutine(newContext, uCont)
172â†’        block.startCoroutineCancellable(coroutine, coroutine)
173â†’        coroutine.getResult()
174â†’    }
175â†’}
176â†’
177â†’/**
178â†’ * Calls the specified suspending block with the given [CoroutineDispatcher], suspends until it
179â†’ * completes, and returns the result.
180â†’ *
181â†’ * This inline function calls [withContext].
182â†’ */
183â†’public suspend inline operator fun <T> CoroutineDispatcher.invoke(
184â†’    noinline block: suspend CoroutineScope.() -> T
185â†’): T = withContext(this, block)
186â†’
187â†’// --------------- implementation ---------------
188â†’
189â†’private open class StandaloneCoroutine(
190â†’    parentContext: CoroutineContext,
191â†’    active: Boolean
192â†’) : AbstractCoroutine<Unit>(parentContext, initParentJob = true, active = active) {
193â†’    override fun handleJobException(exception: Throwable): Boolean {
194â†’        handleCoroutineException(context, exception)
195â†’        return true
196â†’    }
197â†’}
198â†’
199â†’private class LazyStandaloneCoroutine(
200â†’    parentContext: CoroutineContext,
201â†’    block: suspend CoroutineScope.() -> Unit
202â†’) : StandaloneCoroutine(parentContext, active = false) {
203â†’    private val continuation = block.createCoroutineUnintercepted(this, this)
204â†’
205â†’    override fun onStart() {
206â†’        continuation.startCoroutineCancellable(this)
207â†’    }
208â†’}
209â†’
210â†’// Used by withContext when context changes, but dispatcher stays the same
211â†’internal expect class UndispatchedCoroutine<in T>(
212â†’    context: CoroutineContext,
213â†’    uCont: Continuation<T>
214â†’) : ScopeCoroutine<T>
215â†’
216â†’private const val UNDECIDED = 0
217â†’private const val SUSPENDED = 1
218â†’private const val RESUMED = 2
219â†’
220â†’// Used by withContext when context dispatcher changes
221â†’internal class DispatchedCoroutine<in T>(
222â†’    context: CoroutineContext,
223â†’    uCont: Continuation<T>
224â†’) : ScopeCoroutine<T>(context, uCont) {
225â†’    // this is copy-and-paste of a decision state machine inside AbstractionContinuation
226â†’    // todo: we may some-how abstract it via inline class
227â†’    private val _decision = atomic(UNDECIDED)
228â†’
229â†’    private fun trySuspend(): Boolean {
230â†’        _decision.loop { decision ->
231â†’            when (decision) {
232â†’                UNDECIDED -> if (this._decision.compareAndSet(UNDECIDED, SUSPENDED)) return true
233â†’                RESUMED -> return false
234â†’                else -> error("Already suspended")
235â†’            }
236â†’        }
237â†’    }
238â†’
239â†’    private fun tryResume(): Boolean {
240â†’        _decision.loop { decision ->
241â†’            when (decision) {
242â†’                UNDECIDED -> if (this._decision.compareAndSet(UNDECIDED, RESUMED)) return true
243â†’                SUSPENDED -> return false
244â†’                else -> error("Already resumed")
245â†’            }
246â†’        }
247â†’    }
248â†’
249â†’    override fun afterCompletion(state: Any?) {
250â†’        // Call afterResume from afterCompletion and not vice-versa, because stack-size is more
251â†’        // important for afterResume implementation
252â†’        afterResume(state)
253â†’    }
254â†’
255â†’    override fun afterResume(state: Any?) {
256â†’        if (tryResume()) return // completed before getResult invocation -- bail out
257â†’        // Resume in a cancellable way because we have to switch back to the original dispatcher
258â†’        uCont.intercepted().resumeCancellableWith(recoverResult(state, uCont))
259â†’    }
260â†’
261â†’    fun getResult(): Any? {
262â†’        if (trySuspend()) return COROUTINE_SUSPENDED
263â†’        // otherwise, onCompletionInternal was already invoked & invoked tryResume, and the result is in the state
264â†’        val state = this.state.unboxState()
265â†’        if (state is CompletedExceptionally) throw state.cause
266â†’        @Suppress("UNCHECKED_CAST")
267â†’        return state as T
268â†’    }
269â†’}
270â†’
